title: Transport Capsule

description.paragraph1: A futuristic city replaced all means of transport with an intelligent mesh of autonomous capsules.
description.paragraph2: Each vehicle, traffic light, station, and urban drone constantly publishes events: traffic, weather, accidents, maintenance, population flow, and passenger behavior.
description.paragraph3: The systems do not communicate directly. Everything happens through distributed events.
description.paragraph4: New urban services can be added quickly without altering existing systems.
description.paragraph5: The problem is that no one fully understands the system's flow anymore.
description.paragraph6: After a seemingly simple update to the weather forecasting service, capsules started avoiding entire neighborhoods because a weather event was interpreted as a biological threat.

bullet_point.bullet1: New urban modules need to be added without interrupting the rest of the system.
bullet_point.bullet2: Modules must reach the correct locations.
bullet_point.bullet3: Communication flows between systems and route calculation must be fast.
bullet_point.bullet4: Capsules must identify possible risks to user integrity.

footnote: [i]~ Today the capsule is taking longer than ever, 3 hours have passed and no one has answered.[/i]

bug1.description: During a network failure, some warning events were processed twice while others never reached the responsible services. As a result, citizens received transport capsules to destinations from previous trips. Which typical problem of event-driven architecture does this represent?

choices.correct: Difficulty in ensuring consistency and reliability of events
